response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web-focused engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the absence of any Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement explicitly listed as native-level. Beyond that, his technical profile skews heavily toward web, TypeScript, and cloud-native development, with negligible depth in C-based embedded firmware, Linux kernel internals, Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, or any of the security domains specified (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack detection). His brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a university thesis project do not constitute the embedded systems or network-security background this role demands.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career (15+ years) across web, mobile, embedded-adjacent, and infrastructure domains demonstrates broad technical adaptability.
Demonstrated experience with Linux-based environments and low-latency system programming (Sociomantic/DLang async I/O) shows some systems-level sensibility.
Strong DevOps and tooling background (CI/CD, Docker, GitLab, Kubernetes) would be a secondary asset in any engineering team.
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for this role; immediately disqualifying.
No evidence of embedded firmware, network-switch software, Layer-2/Layer-3 protocols, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange).

C programming experience is superficial and dated (a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis); the role demands deep, sustained C/UNIX embedded development expertise.

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking domain experience
